task_completion.description: The 'Volumes' module is currently active in the Slicer interface.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The module selector in the top toolbar displays 'Volumes', or the left control panel is titled 'Volumes'.

task_completion.description: The 'MRHead' volume is selected as the Active Volume.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Volumes module panel, the 'Active Volume' dropdown shows 'MRHead'.

task_completion.description: The 'Display' section within the Volumes module is expanded and visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Display' accordion/group in the left panel is open, revealing display options like 'Lookup Table'.

task_completion.description: A valid color lookup table (LUT) is selected in the dropdown menu.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Lookup Table' dropdown reads a color map name (e.g., 'Ocean', 'Hot', 'Rainbow', 'fMRI') and NOT 'Grey', 'Gray', 'Black', or 'White'.

task_completion.description: The slice views render the brain volume using the selected color map.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The axial, sagittal, and/or coronal slice views show the brain anatomy in colors (e.g., gradients of red/blue/rainbow) corresponding to the selected LUT, instead of grayscale.

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface to make changes.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Volumes module panel and dropdowns, rather than solely typing commands in the Python console.

integrity.description: The screenshots depict the actual 3D Slicer application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The standard 3D Slicer layout (module panel on left, 3D/slice views on right/center) is visible.

integrity.description: The slice views are not obscured by large error messages or unrelated windows.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Red/Yellow/Green slice views are clearly visible to verify the color change.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The initial colormap for 'MRHead' is 'Grey'. Valid target color lookup tables include 'Ocean', 'Cool', 'Warm', 'Hot', 'Cold', 'Rainbow', 'fMRI', 'fMRIPA', 'Spectrum', 'PET-Heat', 'PET-Rainbow', 'Red', 'Green', 'Blue', 'Yellow', 'Cyan', 'Magenta', and 'Labels'. The VLM should confirm the selected value is one of these or clearly a non-grayscale color map.
